title: The One With Joey's New Brain

lines.text: Monica and Chandler's, Monica, Chandler, Ross, and Rachel are sitting around the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm glad you're here, we have a couple of things to ask you about the wedding ceremony.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starting to cry) Ohh...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's the matter? You okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it's just y'know...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) Monica said wedding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, so anyway, we thought one of you could read something during the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! I would love to read a poem.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you think you could get through a poem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (crying) It'll be a short one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so Ross will be doing the reading.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Yeah, I guess, I guess I could do that too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I kind of uh, have something else planned for you guys.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you mind telling us what it is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry, I'm kinda keeping this one on the Q.T.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, whatever it is, I hope it involves winking. (Winks in a spy-type manner.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering, excited) Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So I just talked to one of the DOOL writers today, and...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is DOOL?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Days Of Our Lives. Anyway, you're not gonna believe it! My character is coming out of his coma!!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And-and-and not only that, I'm gettin' a new brain!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So great things are happening at work and in your personal life!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, what do you mean you're getting a new brain?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, they're killing off one of the characters on the show, and when she dies her brain is being transplanted into my body.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? A brain transplant?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (seriously) Yes, it's a highly controversial procedure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's ridiculous!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I think it's ridiculous that you haven't had sex in three and a half months.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Monica and Rachel) It's winter, they are fewer people on the street. (Rachel and Monica smile and nod, knowingly.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who are they killing off?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh Cecilia Monroe, she plays Jessica Lockhart.

lines.speaker: Rachel and Monica
lines.text: Noo!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's my favorite character on DOOL.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She is so good at throwing drinks in people's faces, I mean I don't think I've ever seen her finish a beverage.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And the way she slaps all the time!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wouldn't you love to do it just once?! (Raises her hand towards Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Cecilia Monroe man, what a great actress.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, tell me about it. And she's been on the show forever, it's gonna be really hard to fill her shoes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, help me out here, when you come out of the "brain transplant," you are going to be her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, but in Drake Remoray's body. (Ross laughs unbelievably.) Why is this so hard for you to get? I thought you were a scientist!
Opening Credits
Phoebe and Rachel sitting on the couch in Central Perk

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Clears Throat) Rach, so, that guy there. Straight or gay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (They both look behind them.) Well, I'd have to say gay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah? Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well mainly because he's kissing that other guy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no not that guy there. That guy right there. (Pointing to an attractive man sitting at a table behind them.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah he's too cute to be straight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (A woman with large breasts walks in the door) Ohh knockers will help us figure it out. (She walks by and he checks her out.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, straight, and not subtle. (The man gets up and leaves.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, he left his cell phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, we can hand it to Gunther and he'll put it in lost and found.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or we could use it to call China. See how those guys are doing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What if, um, if he calls his own cell phone to find out who found it and I answer and we start talking and we fell in love. I mean wouldn't that be a great story? Kind of like a fairy tale for the digital age.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah... That does sound great. I'm going to get the phone. (They both get up.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Wait! Why...why do you get the story?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know. I haven't been out on a date in so long.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, you had a date three days ago.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That wasn't a date! That was, that was just friends getting together...(quietly) having sex.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Okay, see? I get the phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No way! No way! You just broke with Tag a week ago.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! (Breaking up) And until now, I didn't think I'd love again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nice try.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh hey-hey wait! How do we fairly decide who gets the phone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know.
They edge closer to the phone on the table.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well umm, maybe we could uhh... (Grabs the phone) Ah-ha! Too slow!!
She holds the phone out and starts taunting Phoebe. Phoebe calming knocks the phone out of Rachel's hand and catches it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah-ha! Too cocky!
Silvercup Studios, Joey is watching Jessica Lockhart perform a scene.

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: I'm going to keep dating him Mother, and there's nothing you can do about it!

lines.speaker: Jessica Lockhart
lines.text: Oh yes there is!

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: What are you going to do? Kill him? Like you did with Charles?!

lines.speaker: Jessica Lockhart
lines.text: (gasps) That was an accident! And so were you.

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: Well, at least I'm not a murderer! (Jessica slaps her.)

lines.speaker: Jessica Lockhart
lines.text: (crying) Oh, my baby! (Hugs Dina, but moves Dina's head to her other shoulder so that she's the only one in the picture.)

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Cut!
Joey walks up to Cecilia Monroe who plays Jessica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That was a great scene! And-and-and that slap looks so real! How do you do that?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Oh, just years of experience.

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: (crying) Can I get some ice here?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh anyway, I just wanted to say how wonderful I think you are.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: You're not the fan who's dying are you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Say what?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I'm supposed to meet and hug a fan whose dying, but that's not supposed to be until (to no one in particular) later!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. No, I'm Joey Tribbiani; we did a scene together yesterday. I-I'm the guy in the coma!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Oh that was a real person?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: An-an-anyway I-I just wanted to say that since I'm getting your brain when you leave the show, I was wondering if there was any tips you can give me...

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I-I-I'm leaving the show?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (quickly) I don't know. Why? Did you hear something?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Who told you that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, one of the writers.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Which one? Was it bald or was it tall?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Y'know what? It doesn't matter! Because it is not true!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: And if it were true, how dare you come to me ask me for tips about a character that I've been playing for 20 years-I'll give you a tip! (She throws her drink in his face.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ms. Monroe... (She slaps him) Oh there you go. (She storms off, leaving Joey standing next to Dina. They share a nod at the ferocity of the slap they just received.)
Central Perk, Phoebe and Rachel are still arguing over the phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Phoebe! You cannot get the phone that way; that's not fair! Okay look, I have an idea. Why don't we, why don't we see what kind of number he has on his speed dial, and then from that we can tell who has more in common with him. And then whoever does gets the phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or, we can decide by whose ever name is closer to the word phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (handing Rachel the phone) Fine all right, but I'd bet you'd be singing another tune if we were fighting over a ratchet.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (checking the speed dial) All right, first name on the speed dial is mom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, I lost my mom to suicide.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay no way, you cannot use that to get the cute guy and the last blueberry muffin.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Did I use that already today? I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes okay. (Checking the speed dial again.) Well now see this isn't telling us anything. (Reading the speed dial) Joe. Carlos. Peter. Ooh! Peter Luger! T hat's a steak house!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, a meat eater. Fine, that's one for you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking at the speed dial) Oh, I win! He's got Barney's on his speed dial.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you don't know that's Barney's the store! That can be y'know his friend's house, or a bar. Who has Barney's the store on their speed dial?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (showing Phoebe her phone) His new girlfriend!
Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are eating lunch, when they start to hear a horrible screeching noise. It sounds like someone is skinning a cat.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think it's the dying cat parade.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It sounds like it's coming from across the street.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (turning around and looking) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know that thing that Ross was gonna do at our wedding?! He was hanging out with me yesterday and he turned to me and said, "You're half Scottish right?"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nooo!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!!
[Cut to Ross's apartment, he his playing the Bagpipes, badly. He's worse than that whole keyboard thing a few years ago.]
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's, scene continued from earlier. They both get up and move to the window.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, there is no way! It can not be Ross! (She looks through the window and sees Ross practicing and fumbling around with the pipes.) Unbelievable! Why is your family Scottish?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why is your family Ross?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He cannot play at our wedding! I mean everyone will leave! I mean come on, that is just noise! It's not even a song!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If you listen very carefully, I think its Celebration by Cool and the Gang.
Phoebe's apartment, Phoebe is putting out some Sunflower seeds as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How are ya?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm Pheebs, remember when we were in the coffee house we decided that I was going to keep the uh, the cute guy's cell phone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And remember how I said I was going to keep it in my purse so that if it rang I could just pick it up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And do you remember going into my purse and stealing the phone?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, now you lost me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You stole the phone!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I didn't!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No? So you're saying that if I called it, it wouldn't ring?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, okay. But while you dial, let me show you the features of my new ringing handbag. (Rachel dials her phone and Phoebe's bag starts to ring.) Oh, it does work! (Rachel grabs the phone and takes it out of Phoebe's handbag.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is a different phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh is it?! (She answers the cute guy's phone.) Uhh, hello? (On her phone) Yes hi, is Rachel there? (On the other phone) Yes she is, just one moment please. (To Phoebe, holding out both phones.) It's for me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is damning evidence.
The cute guy's phone rings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! I bet that's him. My digital fairy tale is about to begin. I wonder how I should be? Should I be uh (In a sexy voice) Hello? Or should I be (Happily) Hi! It's Rach... (Phoebe knocks the phone out of Rachel's hand, catches it, and answers it.) Would you stop doing that?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (on phone) Hello? (Listens) Yes, I'm the one who found your phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe! You can't do th...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) Shhh! I'm on a call! (On phone) Umm well yeah, you can pick it up tonight, say 8:30? At-at my apartment. It's umm, it's umm 5 Morton Street, Apartment 14, umm and then maybe y'know after we can grab a bite to eat or whatever. (Listens) Okay, well okay I'll see you then. (Listens) Bye. (Hangs up.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You do know that I will be here when he comes over.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh? And how will you know what time to come over?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You just said it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Okay, well I-I was kinda hoping that I would just...be alone y'know to think about my mom and her suicide.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! That's the first time today!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! (Exits.)
Silvercup Studios, Dina is at the craft services table getting some food as Joey walks up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you like the nachos uh? Myself I'm partial to...

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: (interrupting) I'm 16.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See you in 2003. (She walks away.)

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: (walking up) You're absolutely right they are writing me out of the show. They don't know exactly when it's going to happen, but apparently going to be very soon and that's it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm so sorry. Look, if it was up to me you would never leave the show.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Yeah, thanks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No I mean it! I can't believe they would do this to you! And to your fans! I mean they are going to be devastated! Heart broken! They love you so much!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Oh you're right. Thank you! What's your name again?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Joey, well thank you. That is so sweet. Oh, excuse me. (She throws her drink on a passing writer.)

lines.speaker: The Writer
lines.text: It wasn't my decision!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: (to him) I'm having a conversation here! (To Joey) You were saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh yeah-ye-ye-ye-ye-ye-l-l-l-l-l-look the-the-the only reason that I, that I came up to you before was because well, I'm really nervous about-about being you. Y'know if you can help me capture the essence of the character. Y'know? Help me keep Jessica alive. Please?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: All right Joey, I will help you. Not because I-I owe it to this stupid show, but because I owe it to Jessica.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh that's great! Oh thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: You're so welcome.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Now, I've been watching some tapes, how's this? (In a British accent.) "Jessica Lockhart will never step foot in this place again! Ever!!"

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Is that supposed to be me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Yeah but Jessica doesn't have an English accent.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shocked) I can do an English accent?! That baby's going on my resume!
Central Perk, Monica and Chandler are sitting on the couch. Ross is sitting on the armchair.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I feel like a snack!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you want some shortbread? Eh that's Scottish like you are.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no thanks. I don't like any thing from my Scottish heritage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well it's just my entire family was run out of Scotland by...Vikings. Anyway, lots of bad memories. (Makes a few unintelligible noises.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well, it sounds to me like your family is ready to uh, rediscover its Scottish roots.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No-no they're not. They're still very angry! But y'know Chandler is also half-Swedish. You know what the Swedish people are famous for? Sitting down and being quiet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well yeah-yeah the Scottish history is so much more...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting) You can not play bagpipes at the wedding!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How did you know about that?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We heard you play all the way from your apartment!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Were you the ones called the cops?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's not really important right now. What is important is; while we appreciate the gesture, we just don't feel bagpipes are appropriate for our wedding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because we hate them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on that's not fair! I mean you haven't even heard me play!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We have heard you play.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, you've heard my practice. Okay? Just-just give me a chance to perform for you and then decide whatever you want. And I'm not going to tell you what song I'm gonna play either. But uh, let's just say when it's over I'll bet there will be a we bit o' celebration.
Silvercup Studios, Joey is getting pointers on how to play Jessica Lockhart.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: So, the essence of the character is rooted in her confidence. So, when Jessica enters a room for instance, she owns everything and every person in that room. (Joey is nodding.) You try.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay! (He scurries out the set door and re-enters, extremely impressed) All right!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: No, he already knows that he owns everything in the room! He's not finding it out for the first time! So, try it again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Okay. (He goes out and comes back in, glaring at everything.)

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Right. He's not angry at the room either. Try it again, he owns it! He owns the room. It is his. He owns, owns, owns, owns the room! He owns it!! (Joey gets a snooty look on his face.) All right, it's a little weird, but it's getting better. (Joey is pleased.) Oh well, I'm gonna miss this woman so much. I don't know what I'm going to do! I mean, it's been 20 years of my life.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well-Hey-hey! Maybe, maybe uh, maybe this is a good thing. Y'know? It'll-it'll give you a chance to shake things up, play different characters. You're so talented.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I am. I am, but I don't know you know. An actor of a certain age is not that easy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey that's not true! Look at uh, look at Angela Lansb-Angelina Jolie!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I probably should've just left years ago when the offers were pouring in, but y'know I just got so comfy here! And... Ohh, I turned down some amazing work!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Like-like what?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Well, let's just say if I left 15 years ago, the landscape of Mexican cinema would be very different today!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (impressed) Wow!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: But... Well now, now's a different time for me. (Starts to cry.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey come on, don't-don't-don't do this! Umm, look let-let me tell you something, okay? Now when I watch you do a scene, I'm thinking, "Boy, she-she is a great actress!" (She's not buying it.) Uh but-but, I am also thinking, "She is hot!"

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: (intrigued) You think I'm hot?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You own the room. (She smiles and stares longingly into his eyes.) We should probably get-get uh...

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Oh yeah-yeah, we should get the... (Pause) So when Jessica kisses a man, she usually puts umm, both her hands on the man's face. (She does so.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, I noticed that! Is that 'cause she's so passionate?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: No! It's because that way the camera only sees her! (She takes her hands off his face.) Do you wanna try it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Okay. (He puts his hands on her face and they kiss.)

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: That was good, that was really good. But I-I think your hands maybe a little off, they should be maybe right like... (She grabs the back of his neck and kisses him passionately causing them to fall onto the couch.)
Commercial Break

Phoebe's apartment, Phoebe and Rachel are waiting for the cute guy to show up for his cell phone. Rachel is putting on perfume by spraying it ahead of her face, and moving into it. Phoebe tries to steal some.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Hey!
There is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: (simultaneously) Who is it?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Hi! It's Tom, I'm here to pick up the phone.
Rachel excitedly jumps up and heads for the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whoa! Why do you get to answer the door?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well why shouldn't I?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because it's my apartment!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, then I get to give him the cell phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. All right. (Hands her the phone.) Good luck explaining all the calls to China.
Phoebe opens the door and Tom, an older gentleman with white hair, enters.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) Wow! How long were we arguing for?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) You're not the man who left the cell phone.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: No that's my assistant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Is-is he coming? (Looks hopefully out the door.)

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Umm, no.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Could you-could you umm, give us one second?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We'll be right back sir.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Sure.
They walk into the living room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wh-what do we do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can you believe this? (Rachel exhales in amazement.) We were waiting for a hot guy and then an even hotter one shows up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know! (Realizes what Phoebe said.) What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm, they just don't make 'em like that anymore!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (turning and looking at Tom again) No-no they do but, you just have to wait.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel, listen-I mean, if you let me have him then I will really owe you one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (fake disappointment) All right. All right Phoebe I will let you have him, but you owe me; you owe me big!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! You're such a great friend!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh...

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: So, which one of you lovely ladies am I going to take to dinner huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that'd be me. Sir. (Hands him the cell phone.) After you.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Okay. Okay. (Exits and Phoebe checks him out.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (whispering) Nice!
Joey and Rachel's, Cecilia is entering the living room from Joey's room followed by Joey.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Well, you certainly own that room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually I rent the whole place and, I just got what you meant. Thank you.
She laughs as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!
Rachel stops dead in her tracks when she sees whom Joey is with.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God!! Ohh, Jessica Lockhart!! In my apartment!! I am such a huge fan! I am such a huge fan!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Well, it's nice to know that you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (screaming) MONICA!!!! MONICA!!!! (Runs to Monica's.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That uh, that is my roommate Rachel.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Oh that explains all the women's underwear.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (shrugs) Sure. Yep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering with Rachel) Oh my God! It's true!! Oh my God you are so amazing! Oh my God, can I just ask you to do me oh, just one favor?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Certainly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Would you slap me? Would you slap me right here in the face?! (Points to her cheek.)

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I'd love to, but my lawyer said I can't do that anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God. You seem really, really nice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean n-not-not fake at all like most famous people.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, here we go. (He grabs them and starts to pull them out of the apartment.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (breaks away) Oh wait, just one more thing! One more minute! (To Cecilia) Umm, you're a stupid bitch.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I really can't slap you. (Monica walks away angrily)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are so beautiful.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nice to meet you! My God you're great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks for stopping by. See ya! (Throws them out and closes the door.) (To Cecilia) I-I am so sorry. I...

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no, being adored. I'm used to it, don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (notices something in the mail that Rachel brought in) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They sent me today's script! They never send the script!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: They don't?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well no, I'm just in a coma. This must mean I have lines! (Realizes what that means.) Oh...

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: How does it happen?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (flipping to the last page) Ew, you get thrown from a horse into an electric fence.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Ah what?! Jessica hates horses!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah well, I'm guessing after this she's not going to be crazy about electricity either.
Monica and Chandler's, Rachel, Phoebe, Chandler, and Monica have gathered to hear Ross perform his interpretation of Celebration by Cool and the Gang on the bag pipe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...now remember you have to imagine me in a kilt.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (giggles) (To Ross) I can imagine you in a short plaid skirt and knee socks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) Do you wanna start telling secrets?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stands up) Now umm, remember I'm still learning.
As he prepares to start, he makes several horrible noises that scares Rachel into retreat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One, two, three, four!

He starts. And well, Celebration was never meant to be played on the bagpipe, so even the best bag pipe players in the world would have trouble with that particular song. So of course, for a beginner like Ross, it sounds absolutely dreadful. The assembled audience minus Phoebe, are horrified. Phoebe, immune to bad music, seems to enjoy it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know the song! Sing along!

Ross resumes playing, this time accompanied by Phoebe screeching out E's in tune with Ross. While Phoebe is singing along, Rachel is having a very difficult time keeping a straight face. Thankfully, Ross gives up after a little while.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Monica and Chandler
lines.text: No!
Ross throws the bagpipes down in disgust.
Silvercup Studios, a scene is being shot where Dina and Fredrick are celebrating Jessica's horrible accident by drinking champagne.

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: Loosening the saddle on mother's horse was brilliant Fredrick. And the electric fence, inspired.

lines.speaker: Fredrick
lines.text: Thank you sweetheart. (They clink glasses.)

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: I can't believe she's really gone. Look around you, all of this is ours. (They move into kiss but; they're stopped by Joey entering with a huge bandage wrapped around his head.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (as Jessica) I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Dina
lines.text: Who are you?!

lines.speaker: Jessica Lockhart
lines.text: What's the matter Dina? Don't you recognize your own (Does a hair flip) mother?!

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Cut! That was great everybody! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: (running out to Joey) That was so wonderful! (Hugs him) Ohh, I think that you're a better Jessica than I ever was!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh noo...

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Well of course not, but you were very good.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: And guess what? Good news! I got another job!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great! Hey! All right! Well-well what is it?!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: A film in Guadalajara!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The airport?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: No that's La Guardia. (Joey nods in recognition.) This is Mexico.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh. Wow! Well how-how, how will you be gone?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Eight months.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's a really long time.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Yeah, but you can come and visit me. I bet that you could uh, own a few places down there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I tell ya, I should probably buy a place in the city first. (Realizes.) And I just got what you meant again-That is-I tell ya, that is a tricky one!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: That is a tricky one. Well, Joey I really wanna thank you. You've, well you made a very difficult time for me a little less painful.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: You too.
They kiss and both put their hands on the other's face like Jessica would do.
Ending Credits

Monica and Chandler's, Rachel, Phoebe, Chandler, and Monica have gathered to hear Ross perform his interpretation of Celebration by Cool and the Gang on the bag pipe. Yes, I typed that earlier. We're seeing this again, only this time Ross as already started playing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know the song! Sing along!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Eeee!!! Eee!! Eee!!

Monica (Courtney Cox) and Chandler (Matthew Perry) are laughing. That then causes Lisa and Jennifer come out of character and start laughing hysterically. And that finally causes David Schwimmer to come out of character and start laughing as well. Matthew decides to sing along now as well.

lines.speaker: Matthew Perry
lines.text: Eee!! (This causes more laughter.)

lines.speaker: Lisa Kudrow
lines.text: Do it again!
Matthew mimics the sound again.
End

Why was this the trailer? Well, that's because it was an introduction into the special out takes episode that immediately followed the show. The entire out takes episode, Friends: The Stuff You've Never Seen can be read by following this link.

lines.speaker: Friends
lines.text: The Stuff You've Never Seen

lines.speaker: Hosted by
lines.text: Conan O'Brien
This is a special out takes episode. The cast and Conan are sitting around the set of Central Perk, talking about the stuff we've never seen.
Central Perk, the cast of Friends along with Conan O'Brien are sitting and talking.

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: It's a tradition here on Friends after every taping for me to hang out with you guys, (They all laugh) talk down the episode umm... The point of this whole thing is what people see in America is: they see Friends, they love the show, it looks like a smooth running machine, but behind the scenes there's deceit, mistrust, and hate. And I thought, I thought we'd actually take a look at uh, y'know some of these moments where you guys are-there are mistakes. You make mistakes.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Once and a while.

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: From time to time.

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: For example, I don't have to memorize lines. You guys actually have to remember what to say and you probably forget from time to time. Yes?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Our energy just comes way up when there's an audience here and when that happens, something happens between your brain and your mouth sometimes and it just doesn't, it just doesn't work.
[Cut to Central Perk, Ross, Phoebe, Monica, and Chandler are there. I think it's The One With The Joke.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, oh-oh, umm no you didn't. I did.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh uh-uh, no-no-no-no-uh-uh. (He starts laughing, causing everyone else to laugh.)
[Cut to Joey and Rachel's, Phoebe is talking. It looks like when Rachel and Monica lived in this apartment.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, we realize that-Oh no... (She resets herself) I'm telling it! I'm telling it... (She loses it.)
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Joey is talking to Monica and Chandler.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ha-ha. Look-Come on, I don't know what to do...or say. (He laughs.)
[Reset]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ha-ha, very funny. I don't know what to do! Y'know? Holy crud!
[Reset]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ha-ha-ha, very funny. Look, I don't know what to do! (Long pause, as everyone cracks up.)

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: It is one of those days!
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Phoebe is speaking Italian to Joey's grandmother. I'm spelling phonetically.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 'Xcusa seniora, voulez-bere quakay-[Beep]-uck it!

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: Wow Pheebs, you-you speak gutter?
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: You still get nervous everybody just before a show?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: Everybody.

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: It's amazing like all week long we've-we've been saying the same lines and then the audience is here and we will mess up, and if you mess up once, then you'll get nervous because you'll-you know you'll probably mess up again.
[Cut to Central Perk, first season Monica is talking.]

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Action!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (holding her hand in front of her face) When you were little you slept through-through the Grand Canyon.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Watch again that hand.

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: This'll be five/ten takes.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: Okay. You know it's gonna happen.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Once again, and action!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (the hand's still there) When-when you were little you slept through the Grand Canyon. (She actually itches her nose this time.)

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: Oh! Okay! I'm gonna try it without the coffee cup 'cause I think it's the left hand that's messing me up.
[Reset]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When you were little you slept through the Grand-(Pointing again)-Oh fffff....
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Phoebe are talking.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sorry, let's go back! 'Cause you've got more to say.

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: Do I? Absolutely. Absolutely. Yeah, that's your fault. I...say.
They both laugh.

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: I love you! (They hug.)
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: But there must be, there must-are a lot of moments over the years where you're just trying to do your job, something goes wrong.
[Cut to Joey and Rachel's, Joey is giving Chandler the bracelet from season 2.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey and, this is a little extra something for y'know, always being there for me.
Suddenly there's a noise off stage and the camera on Joey swings around.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Hey Joey! The camera hit our wall!

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: What?!
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Chandler and Joey are living here and Ross is writing on the Magna-Doodle when Rachel opens the door causing the door knob to hit his hand.]

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Ahh! Sh-(Beep)-it that hurt!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Are you okay?
[Reset]
[When he hears Jennifer try to open the door he jumps back, and Jennifer is unable to open the door this time.]
[Cut the hallway, Rachel is exited from Monica's when the door closes on her skirt.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Rach! (She breaks up and goes back into Monica's.)
[Cut to Joey and Rachel's, with the giant entertainment center Joey is exiting from his room.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah? Then how come I keep-(He notices that the marker board they use has been left on the entertainment center and holds up his discovery.)
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, first season, Monica is making a giant sub-sandwich and is talking to Rachel. I think it's The One With Fake Monica.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was so wild! We told 'em we were the Gunderson's in 16...
Jennifer starts laughing.

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: (laughing) I spit on her!
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: (to Lisa) Operation. You had a fun one.

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: With Operation.

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: It was a little game. Yeah, with an electric buzzer.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, The One With George Stephanopoulos, Phoebe is showing Monica and Rachel that she brought Operation to their slumber party.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh-ooh, and I brought Operation, but umm I lost the umm... (It starts buzzing) It's making a noise.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: But le Blanc really doesn't mess up much.

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: You don't verbally mess up, but sometimes physically? You mess up.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: I have had some clumsy moments I guess you can call 'em.

[Cut to Central Perk, to the theme from The Dick Van Dyke show Joey runs into Central Perk carrying a stack of Soap Opera Digests and falls on the step. He does bounce right back up making it all that much funnier.]
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: He fell down once! And we re-did it and we went back. And he-(laughs)-he was afraid he was gonna fall down...

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: You could actually see him trying not to fall down.
[Cut to Central Perk, same as before Joey is entering.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs! (He looks down as he goes down the step to make sure he didn't fall again.) Check it out! (He starts laughing when he realized what he did.)
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: This particular time when he continued to fall or y'know, try not to fall, I was in the room with Matthew and Matthew was like, "Should I do it?"
[Reset from before, Matt doesn't fall or look down.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs! Check it out! Check it out! Check it out! Check it out! (Hands her one.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, Soap Opera Digest!
As she's saying that Joey is to pull out a chair and sit down, only Matthew comes running in from off camera and dives for the same chair.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: Matthew, you have a reputation with the rest of the cast that sometimes you like to, you like to fool around a bit. I mean like if something's naturally going wrong you like to get in there and juice it a little bit. True or false?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: I don't necessarily like to juice things...

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: He said true or false!
[Cut to Monica's restaurant kitchen, it's the episode where Joey is working as a waiter at Monica's restaurant. Joey is patting her breast from when she set it on fire.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Ooh-ooh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're still a tiny bit on fire there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh geez! Okay! Thanks!
Matthew runs in and starts patting the other breast, then walks away. Matt slowly stops patting her breast.
[Cut to that same kitchen, only this is The One With The Proposal, Richard is telling Monica something.]

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: It was great seeing you the other night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it was good to see you too. Did you come down here to tell me that?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.text: Noo! I came down here to tell you something else. I came here to tell you I still love you.
Suddenly the door flies open and in walks Chandler!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What the fu-(beep)-ck are you doing?!
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: Now you guys work with animals a lot. You had to work early on with a monkey...

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: That damn monkey.

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: That damn monkey.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, The One Where The Monkey Gets Away, Rachel is watching a soap opera with Marcel.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Okay, see now the one with the feather boa? That's Dr. Francis. She used to be a man. Oh look! There-(Marcel (Katie) jumps away)-Okay. (And runs behind her on the back of the couch for a little while.)

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Katie. Geez! See Katie, come here-Katie! (Katie spills some popcorn.) Katie, come here Katie. (She obeys and sits where she's supposed to.) Very good.
[Reset, they're about to start the scene when Katie suddenly jumps up startling Jennifer.]
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Lisa's laugh though...There's-It's so infectious. It's one of those things...just forget about it. Once it starts, it's all gone.
[Cut to Joey and Rachel's, it's actually Joey and Rachel's. Phoebe is trying to convince Rachel to switch with her and live with Monica as Joey looks on.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No y'know, I don't want to switch! Come on! I can throw wet paper towels here!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well at Monica's you can eat-(Suddenly cracks up.)

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: Okay, shh yeah. 'Cause it's not silly.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Here we go.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: And...action!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no Ph-Ph-Ph-Ph... (She starts laughing.)
[Reset]

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: (to Lisa) Ready?

lines.speaker: Lisa
lines.text: Yeah.
They all start laughing.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: And then it just builds on itself and there's no doing the scene after. I mean you probably wait and really get it together and do it.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: For me, I have a hard time with le Blanc in particular. When-when-I mean when...

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: (to Matt) You bastard.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: Yeah, what did I do?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: ...I keep a straight face he-he delivers like this look, a reaction to you, or a certain take, I-I-I mean I find it so funny.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Ross is eating breakfast with Joey and Monica. Joey is walking towards Ross.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mornin'

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mornin'
Joey hugs Ross's neck and has a look of complete contentment on his face which, after a short while, causes David and Matt to start laughing.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Monica is throwing a party. Joey is talking to Ross about the bad audition he just had while pouring booze onto a snow cone.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean what kind of an actor, what kind of an actor can't even say, "Hmm, noodle soup?"
David smiles.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: That's a good one? (They both laugh.)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Now, we should go back and take the other line.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: Okay, I'll just put a little more booze on there. (Pours some more on.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think, subconsciously...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (cracking up) Nope, you lost me.
[Reset, Joey is about to pour more booze on.]

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: No! Come on!
[Cut to Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Ross are giving Phoebe and Rachel the bride's maid test.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, the next situation is for Rachel. The wedding is about to start you walk into the back room and you find Monica taking a nap with Ross. (Ross lies on the floor.) I'll be Monica. Go! (He jumps down and cuddles up with Ross.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (jumping up) No! No! No!
David and Matt just lose it then.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: We'll be right back with more Friends, less me.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Courtney and Matthew are getting ready to do a scene where Monica's sick.]

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: I'm doing my brother.

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: That's...gross.
Commercial Break
[Conan and the cast in Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: You uh, you've worked with-They always say a performer should never work with pets or children.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Aww, the kids.
[Cut to Carol and Susan's apartment, from next week's episode Rachel is talking to Ben.]

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Action.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Now-What is my first line?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: (prompting her) What did we agree?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Okay...
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: The good thing about the young kids though, they're completely unpredictable. Which is a lot of fun as an actor to respond with. But there was one story...

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Oh little Ben.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Where the story was that I was anticipating that I would be around to hear my son's first words spoken. But the scene was about that he wasn't supposed to be able to speak and, uh for some reason when we started doing the show...
[Cut to Central Perk, Ross is taking Ben to visit Rachel who's working there.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! We're visiting!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's Ben and his Da-Da. Da-Da? Can you say Da-Da? Y'know, you might as well say it because I told your...

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Da...Da.
[Reset]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can you say Da-Da? See, I'm gonna tell your mommies you said it anyway, so you might as well try...

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Da...Da.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Da...Da.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, he's saying Da-Da.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: And then sometimes during the show y'know but you're like, the scene's going one way but you're just tempted to say something another time. Like, do you remember that one where Monica's baking cookies in our old apartment?

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: She's trying to intrigue us to hang out with her.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: Yeah, she's trying to waft the smell across the hall to get us to come hang out in her new place, and we're sitting there eating pizza and I think it was you (Points to Lisa) that said...
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, the scene described above.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do I smell?
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: I think I was supposed to say, "I don't know," and go over and open the door. And I went...
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, same scene.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do I smell?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Pause) Sorry. (Raises his hand in shame.)
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: (everyone laughs) And then it was like four takes later before we could get through it with a straight face.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, same scene.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do I smell?
Pause.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Le Blanc.
[Reset]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do I smell?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, but it smells good. (He gets up and heads for the door only to stop short and start laughing.)
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: But audiences-You have a live studio audience and they must love that. They must love it when they see you guys playing.
[Cut to Joey and Rachel's, Ross is living with Chandler and Joey. Joey and Ross have built a fort out of boxes, Chandler enters and they stand up slowly.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you guys doing?
Ross and Joey both reach down and pull up their pants.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothin'.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: So that you will intentionally do something that-they'll-they'll intentionally screw it up?

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah!
[Cut to Monica and Ross leaving Joey and Chandler's hotel room in London. As they exit Joey and Chandler enter from the bathroom with both of their pants down around their ankles.]

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: (noticing the laughter) Why? What's the matter?
They both bounce back into the bathroom.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: (to Courtney) You-you've worn a fat suit on the show. And, a lot of people love you in the fat suit. Do you like wearing the fat suit? Is it fun?
[Cut to Central Perk, Fat Monica and Rachel are on the couch.]

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: I'm melting!
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: They made me dance, in the fat suit.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Courtney is dancing in the fat suit and after shaking her groove thing sits down in exhaustion.]
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: I-I heard some of you guys talking about this earlier, but sometimes there's just a word that someone has to say that you'll get hung up on. And it'll just-the way you say the word is funny to everybody else.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, is the one where Rachel screwed up the desert and Ross and Joey are trying to enjoy it.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That tastes like feet!
David puts his napkin up to his mouth and starts laughing at his own line. Matt notices him after a while and starts laughing as well.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: It tastes like (mimicking him in a high-pitched voice) feet!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Okay, we're good.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Action!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That tastes like feet!
Matt starts laughing.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: Sometimes the dialogue itself is just so funny and you'll-we'll be rehearsing during the week and you just-whatever-whatever the joke is; it's so funny we can't get through it in rehearsal and just...

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: You just know.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: You-you mentally make a flag on it and you say, "Okay show night, I'm just-I'll never be able to get through this."
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Sick Monica is trying to entice Chandler to have sex with her.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you saying that you don't want to get with this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You see, I don't say-(Starts laughing.)
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, it's the one with the fake chocolate. Monica has baked some cookies and Phoebe is trying them.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (spitting the cookie out onto a napkin) Oh, sweet Je-(Beep)-sus! Oh! Monica, these are the (laughing) cookies they serve in hell!
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, the gang is watching Joey's debut on Days.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (noticing the woman on the screen) Whoa! She's purty!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, and she's really nice too! She taught me about y'know, how to work with the cameras and smell-the-fart acting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me, what?
Matt turns back and looks and them, but instead of his next line he starts laughing.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: When you have to do physical business for a scene, I mean there must be; there must be a lot of funny moments when you have to physically do a task as part of a scene.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, Rachel, Chandler, and Ross had to try to get a couch up a stair-a very narrow New York stairwell and that was probably...I-I think it was the hardest I've-I've laughed in my life period.
[Cut to the scene described above.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pivot! (They pivot) Pivot! (They pivot) Okay, pivot! Piv-at! (He starts laughing.)
[Reset]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pivot! Piv-at! Piv-at!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Shut up! Shut up! Shut up!!
David is laughing.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Pivat!! (In a high pitched voice) Pivat!!!
Commercial Break
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: You've done over 150 episodes, but your favorite moments that 80 years from now you'll be thinking about?

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: It is when we're able to crack each other up.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is cleaning the apartment for Monica and is frustrated with things not lining up.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica's gotta have the phone in the right place and-(Frantic babbling.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Chandler) All right! All right! All right! (To Joey) We are fixing it.
Matt starts laughing.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: He's gone. He's...
Matt mimics Chandler.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: We enjoy watching each other. And I settle for watching each other's performance, and we like each other.
[Cut to Ross's second wedding reception, Joey has just told him the band is ready with Rachel looking on.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh the band's ready! And well-I-I-we gotta do what the band says, right? I don't care about the stinkin' band!!
Jennifer starts laughing.
[Cut back to the cast and Conan.]

lines.speaker: Conan
lines.text: Okay Friends gang, thanks for doing it.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well thank you so much.

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Thank you Conan!

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Well thank for coming here, it's good to see you.
[Cut to Central Perk, Phoebe at the mike.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you my babies. (Waves good-bye.)
Ending Credits
[Cut to Monica's work kitchen, she's on fire again and Joey is putting her out.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh what are doing?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're still a tiny bit on fire there!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think you got it!

lines.speaker: Courtney
lines.text: I'm sorry, I messed up.

lines.speaker: Matt
lines.text: I'll do it again and again if you want.
[Cut to Monica and Chandler's, the bad desert Ross is quickly eating the mound on his plate.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no! I'll-I'll... (He takes too much and some falls out of his mouth, which starts him laughing.)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: That is too much!
Matt grabs his plate and takes some of what's on Ross's plate.
[We close with a bunch of scenes where they screw up and make weird noises. It finishes with.]

lines.speaker: Matthew
lines.text: Let me start that again.
End
